assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 7: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' — matches the fixture values exactly.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 8: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — new entry present with serena_ui as the Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 12-16 contain all 5 fields matching the fixture: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has '## Preserved (Existing)' section (lines 3-25) listing trustify-backend, Jira, Code Intelligence, and Bug Configuration as preserved, and '## Added (New)' section (lines 27-33) listing trustify-ui and serena_ui limitation as added.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 9: 'serena_backend — Already configured in Repository Registry (trustify-backend). No action needed.' and line 10: 'serena_ui — Newly discovered, NOT yet in the Repository Registry.'

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md preserves: naming convention (line 20: 'Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name'), example using serena_backend (lines 25-29), serena_backend limitation (line 33: 'rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). Only new content is line 34: 'serena_ui: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 37-40: '## Bug Configuration' with Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks — all matching fixture. discovery-log.md line 38: 'Bug Configuration is up to date — no changes needed.'

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 42: 'Security Configuration opt-in offered (no existing Security Configuration section detected). User declined to enable security triage.' changes-log.md lines 35-38 confirm under '## Declined': 'User declined to enable security triage for this project.' claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section.
